# The Google API surface

<!-- GENERATED FILE — DO NOT EDIT. Run `npm run generate-api-surface`. -->

Every method Google publishes for the APIs this server targets, and whether we expose it — plus the APIs we do **not** target yet, so you can see what is on the table and ask for it. Descriptions are **Google's own**, quoted verbatim from the [Discovery documents](https://developers.google.com/discovery) — the same specification the client is generated from. They are not paraphrased.

**Missing something?** Click **Request** on the method. It opens a pre-filled issue. Read **[API coverage](coverage.md)** first — it explains what makes a request persuasive.

| `calendars.insert`<br>*POST* | Creates a secondary calendar. The authenticated user for the request is made the data owner of the new calendar. Note: We recommend to authenticate as the intended data owner of the calendar. You can use domain-wide delegation of authority to allow applications to act on behalf of a specific user. Don't use a service account for authentication. If you use a service account for authentication, the service account is the data owner, which can lead to unexpected behavior. For example, if a service account is the data owner, data ownership cannot be transferred. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20calendar.calendars.insert&body=**Method%3A**%20%60calendar.calendars.insert%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20secondary%20calendar.%20The%20authenticated%20user%20for%20the%20request%20is%20made%20the%20data%20owner%20of%20the%20new%20calendar.%20Note%3A%20We%20recommend%20to%20authenticate%20as%20the%20intended%20data%20owner%20of%20the%20calendar.%20You%20can%20use%20domain-wide%20delegation%20of%20authority%20to%20allow%20applications%20to%20act%20on%20behalf%20of%20a%20specific%20user.%20Don't%20use%20a%20service%20account%20for%20authentication.%20If%20you%20use%20a%20service%20account%20for%20authentication%2C%20the%20service%20account%20is%20the%20data%20owner%2C%20which%20can%20lead%20to%20unexpected%20behavior.%20For%20example%2C%20if%20a%20service%20account%20is%20the%20data%20owner%2C%20data%20ownership%20cannot%20be%20transferred.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `calendars.transferOwnership`<br>*POST* | Transfers a secondary calendar between users within a Google Workspace organization. Requires user authentication with Manage Calendars administrator privilege, and one of the following authorization scopes: - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.calendars In the request, set useAdminAccess to true. The secondary calendar must be active to be transferred. Transferring disabled or deleted calendars isn't supported. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20calendar.calendars.transferOwnership&body=**Method%3A**%20%60calendar.calendars.transferOwnership%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Transfers%20a%20secondary%20calendar%20between%20users%20within%20a%20Google%20Workspace%20organization.%20Requires%20user%20authentication%20with%20Manage%20Calendars%20administrator%20privilege%2C%20and%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fcalendar%20-%20https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fcalendar.calendars%20In%20the%20request%2C%20set%20useAdminAccess%20to%20true.%20The%20secondary%20calendar%20must%20be%20active%20to%20be%20transferred.%20Transferring%20disabled%20or%20deleted%20calendars%20isn't%20supported.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

## docs

`manage_docs` — 3 of 3 methods exposed. Google Discovery: `docs:v1`.

| Method | | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `documents.batchUpdate`<br>*POST* | Applies one or more updates to the document. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is not valid, then the entire request will fail and nothing will be applied. Some requests have replies to give you some information about how they are applied. Other requests do not need to return information; these each return an empty reply. The order of replies matches that of the requests. For example, suppose you call batchUpdate with four updates, and only the third one returns information. The response would have two empty replies, the reply to the third request, and another empty reply, in that order. Because other users may be editing the document, the document might not exactly reflect your changes: your changes may be altered with respect to collaborator changes. If there are no collaborators, the document should reflect your changes. In any case, the updates in your request are guaranteed to be applied together atomically. | ✅ `manage_docs write, insertText, replaceText` |
| `documents.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a blank document using the title given in the request. Other fields in the request, including any provided content, are ignored. Returns the created document. | ✅ `manage_docs create` |
| `documents.get`<br>*GET* | Gets the latest version of the specified document. | ✅ `manage_docs get` |

| `approvals.reassign`<br>*POST* | Reassigns the reviewers on an approval. For more information, see Manage approvals. Adds or replaces reviewers in the ReviewerResponse of the approval. This can be called by any user with the `writer` permission on the file while the approval Status is `IN_PROGRESS` and the Response for the reviewer being reassigned is `NO_RESPONSE`. A user with the `reader` permission can only reassign an approval that's assigned to themselves. Removing a reviewer isn't allowed. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20drive.approvals.reassign&body=**Method%3A**%20%60drive.approvals.reassign%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Reassigns%20the%20reviewers%20on%20an%20approval.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Manage%20approvals.%20Adds%20or%20replaces%20reviewers%20in%20the%20ReviewerResponse%20of%20the%20approval.%20This%20can%20be%20called%20by%20any%20user%20with%20the%20%60writer%60%20permission%20on%20the%20file%20while%20the%20approval%20Status%20is%20%60IN_PROGRESS%60%20and%20the%20Response%20for%20the%20reviewer%20being%20reassigned%20is%20%60NO_RESPONSE%60.%20A%20user%20with%20the%20%60reader%60%20permission%20can%20only%20reassign%20an%20approval%20that's%20assigned%20to%20themselves.%20Removing%20a%20reviewer%20isn't%20allowed.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `files.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a file. For more information, see Create and manage files. This method supports an */upload* URI and accepts uploaded media with the following characteristics: - *Maximum file size:* 5,120 GB - *Accepted Media MIME types:* `*/*` (Specify a valid MIME type, rather than the literal `*/*` value. The literal `*/*` is only used to indicate that any valid MIME type can be uploaded. For more information, see Google Workspace and Google Drive supported MIME types.) For more information on uploading files, see Upload file data. Apps creating shortcuts with the `create` method must specify the MIME type `application/vnd.google-apps.shortcut`. Apps should specify a file extension in the `name` property when inserting files with the API. For example, an operation to insert a JPEG file should specify something like `"name": "cat.jpg"` in the metadata. Subsequent `GET` requests include the read-only `fileExtension` property populated with the extension originally specified in the `name` property. When a Google Drive user requests to download a file, or when the file is downloaded through the sync client, Drive builds a full filename (with extension) based on the name. In cases where the extension is missing, Drive attempts to determine the extension based on the file's MIME type. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20drive.files.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60drive.files.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20file.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Create%20and%20manage%20files.%20This%20method%20supports%20an%20*%2Fupload*%20URI%20and%20accepts%20uploaded%20media%20with%20the%20following%20characteristics%3A%20-%20*Maximum%20file%20size%3A*%205%2C120%20GB%20-%20*Accepted%20Media%20MIME%20types%3A*%20%60*%2F*%60%20(Specify%20a%20valid%20MIME%20type%2C%20rather%20than%20the%20literal%20%60*%2F*%60%20value.%20The%20literal%20%60*%2F*%60%20is%20only%20used%20to%20indicate%20that%20any%20valid%20MIME%20type%20can%20be%20uploaded.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Google%20Workspace%20and%20Google%20Drive%20supported%20MIME%20types.)%20For%20more%20information%20on%20uploading%20files%2C%20see%20Upload%20file%20data.%20Apps%20creating%20shortcuts%20with%20the%20%60create%60%20method%20must%20specify%20the%20MIME%20type%20%60application%2Fvnd.google-apps.shortcut%60.%20Apps%20should%20specify%20a%20file%20extension%20in%20the%20%60name%60%20property%20when%20inserting%20files%20with%20the%20API.%20For%20example%2C%20an%20operation%20to%20insert%20a%20JPEG%20file%20should%20specify%20something%20like%20%60%22name%22%3A%20%22cat.jpg%22%60%20in%20the%20metadata.%20Subsequent%20%60GET%60%20requests%20include%20the%20read-only%20%60fileExtension%60%20property%20populated%20with%20the%20extension%20originally%20specified%20in%20the%20%60name%60%20property.%20When%20a%20Google%20Drive%20user%20requests%20to%20download%20a%20file%2C%20or%20when%20the%20file%20is%20downloaded%20through%20the%20sync%20client%2C%20Drive%20builds%20a%20full%20filename%20(with%20extension)%20based%20on%20the%20name.%20In%20cases%20where%20the%20extension%20is%20missing%2C%20Drive%20attempts%20to%20determine%20the%20extension%20based%20on%20the%20file's%20MIME%20type.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `files.update`<br>*PATCH* | Updates a file's metadata, content, or both. When calling this method, only populate fields in the request that you want to modify. When updating fields, some fields might be changed automatically, such as `modifiedDate`. This method supports patch semantics. This method supports an */upload* URI and accepts uploaded media with the following characteristics: - *Maximum file size:* 5,120 GB - *Accepted Media MIME types:* `*/*` (Specify a valid MIME type, rather than the literal `*/*` value. The literal `*/*` is only used to indicate that any valid MIME type can be uploaded. For more information, see Google Workspace and Google Drive supported MIME types.) | `users.messages.import`<br>*POST* | Imports a message into only this user's mailbox, with standard email delivery scanning and classification similar to receiving via SMTP. This method doesn't perform SPF checks, so it might not work for some spam messages, such as those attempting to perform domain spoofing. This method does not send a message. Note that the maximum size of the message is 150 MB. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.messages.import&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.messages.import%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Imports%20a%20message%20into%20only%20this%20user's%20mailbox%2C%20with%20standard%20email%20delivery%20scanning%20and%20classification%20similar%20to%20receiving%20via%20SMTP.%20This%20method%20doesn't%20perform%20SPF%20checks%2C%20so%20it%20might%20not%20work%20for%20some%20spam%20messages%2C%20such%20as%20those%20attempting%20to%20perform%20domain%20spoofing.%20This%20method%20does%20not%20send%20a%20message.%20Note%20that%20the%20maximum%20size%20of%20the%20message%20is%20150%20MB.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `users.settings.cse.identities.create`<br>*POST* | Creates and configures a client-side encryption identity that's authorized to send mail from the user account. Google publishes the S/MIME certificate to a shared domain-wide directory so that people within a Google Workspace organization can encrypt and send mail to the identity. For administrators managing identities and keypairs for users in their organization, requests require authorization with a service account that has domain-wide delegation authority to impersonate users with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.settings.basic` scope. For users managing their own identities and keypairs, requests require hardware key encryption turned on and configured. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.cse.identities.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.cse.identities.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20and%20configures%20a%20client-side%20encryption%20identity%20that's%20authorized%20to%20send%20mail%20from%20the%20user%20account.%20Google%20publishes%20the%20S%2FMIME%20certificate%20to%20a%20shared%20domain-wide%20directory%20so%20that%20people%20within%20a%20Google%20Workspace%20organization%20can%20encrypt%20and%20send%20mail%20to%20the%20identity.%20For%20administrators%20managing%20identities%20and%20keypairs%20for%20users%20in%20their%20organization%2C%20requests%20require%20authorization%20with%20a%20service%20account%20that%20has%20domain-wide%20delegation%20authority%20to%20impersonate%20users%20with%20the%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fgmail.settings.basic%60%20scope.%20For%20users%20managing%20their%20own%20identities%20and%20keypairs%2C%20requests%20require%20hardware%20key%20encryption%20turned%20on%20and%20configured.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `users.settings.cse.keypairs.disable`<br>*POST* | Turns off a client-side encryption key pair. The authenticated user can no longer use the key pair to decrypt incoming CSE message texts or sign outgoing CSE mail. To regain access, use the EnableCseKeyPair to turn on the key pair. After 30 days, you can permanently delete the key pair by using the ObliterateCseKeyPair method. For administrators managing identities and keypairs for users in their organization, requests require authorization with a service account that has domain-wide delegation authority to impersonate users with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.settings.basic` scope. For users managing their own identities and keypairs, requests require hardware key encryption turned on and configured. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.disable&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.disable%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Turns%20off%20a%20client-side%20encryption%20key%20pair.%20The%20authenticated%20user%20can%20no%20longer%20use%20the%20key%20pair%20to%20decrypt%20incoming%20CSE%20message%20texts%20or%20sign%20outgoing%20CSE%20mail.%20To%20regain%20access%2C%20use%20the%20EnableCseKeyPair%20to%20turn%20on%20the%20key%20pair.%20After%2030%20days%2C%20you%20can%20permanently%20delete%20the%20key%20pair%20by%20using%20the%20ObliterateCseKeyPair%20method.%20For%20administrators%20managing%20identities%20and%20keypairs%20for%20users%20in%20their%20organization%2C%20requests%20require%20authorization%20with%20a%20service%20account%20that%20has%20domain-wide%20delegation%20authority%20to%20impersonate%20users%20with%20the%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fgmail.settings.basic%60%20scope.%20For%20users%20managing%20their%20own%20identities%20and%20keypairs%2C%20requests%20require%20hardware%20key%20encryption%20turned%20on%20and%20configured.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

For users managing their own identities and keypairs, requests require hardware key encryption turned on and configured. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.get&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.get%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Retrieves%20an%20existing%20client-side%20encryption%20key%20pair.%20For%20administrators%20managing%20identities%20and%20keypairs%20for%20users%20in%20their%20organization%2C%20requests%20require%20authorization%20with%20a%20service%20account%20that%20has%20domain-wide%20delegation%20authority%20to%20impersonate%20users%20with%20the%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fgmail.settings.basic%60%20scope.%20For%20users%20managing%20their%20own%20identities%20and%20keypairs%2C%20requests%20require%20hardware%20key%20encryption%20turned%20on%20and%20configured.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `users.settings.cse.keypairs.list`<br>*GET* | Lists client-side encryption key pairs for an authenticated user. For administrators managing identities and keypairs for users in their organization, requests require authorization with a service account that has domain-wide delegation authority to impersonate users with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.settings.basic` scope. For users managing their own identities and keypairs, requests require hardware key encryption turned on and configured. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.list&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.list%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Lists%20client-side%20encryption%20key%20pairs%20for%20an%20authenticated%20user.%20For%20administrators%20managing%20identities%20and%20keypairs%20for%20users%20in%20their%20organization%2C%20requests%20require%20authorization%20with%20a%20service%20account%20that%20has%20domain-wide%20delegation%20authority%20to%20impersonate%20users%20with%20the%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fgmail.settings.basic%60%20scope.%20For%20users%20managing%20their%20own%20identities%20and%20keypairs%2C%20requests%20require%20hardware%20key%20encryption%20turned%20on%20and%20configured.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `users.settings.cse.keypairs.obliterate`<br>*POST* | Deletes a client-side encryption key pair permanently and immediately. You can only permanently delete key pairs that have been turned off for more than 30 days. To turn off a key pair, use the DisableCseKeyPair method. Gmail can't restore or decrypt any messages that were encrypted by an obliterated key. Authenticated users and Google Workspace administrators lose access to reading the encrypted messages. For administrators managing identities and keypairs for users in their organization, requests require authorization with a service account that has domain-wide delegation authority to impersonate users with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.settings.basic` scope. For users managing their own identities and keypairs, requests require hardware key encryption turned on and configured. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.obliterate&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.cse.keypairs.obliterate%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Deletes%20a%20client-side%20encryption%20key%20pair%20permanently%20and%20immediately.%20You%20can%20only%20permanently%20delete%20key%20pairs%20that%20have%20been%20turned%20off%20for%20more%20than%2030%20days.%20To%20turn%20off%20a%20key%20pair%2C%20use%20the%20DisableCseKeyPair%20method.%20Gmail%20can't%20restore%20or%20decrypt%20any%20messages%20that%20were%20encrypted%20by%20an%20obliterated%20key.%20Authenticated%20users%20and%20Google%20Workspace%20administrators%20lose%20access%20to%20reading%20the%20encrypted%20messages.%20For%20administrators%20managing%20identities%20and%20keypairs%20for%20users%20in%20their%20organization%2C%20requests%20require%20authorization%20with%20a%20service%20account%20that%20has%20domain-wide%20delegation%20authority%20to%20impersonate%20users%20with%20the%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fgmail.settings.basic%60%20scope.%20For%20users%20managing%20their%20own%20identities%20and%20keypairs%2C%20requests%20require%20hardware%20key%20encryption%20turned%20on%20and%20configured.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `users.settings.delegates.create`<br>*POST* | Adds a delegate with its verification status set directly to `accepted`, without sending any verification email. The delegate user must be a member of the same Google Workspace organization as the delegator user. For more information, see Manage delegates. Gmail imposes limitations on the number of delegates and delegators each user in a Google Workspace organization can have. These limits depend on your organization, but in general each user can have up to 25 delegates and up to 10 delegators. A delegate user must be referred to by their primary email address, and not an email alias. When a new delegate is created, there may be up to a one minute delay before the new delegate is available for use. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.delegates.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.delegates.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Adds%20a%20delegate%20with%20its%20verification%20status%20set%20directly%20to%20%60accepted%60%2C%20without%20sending%20any%20verification%20email.%20The%20delegate%20user%20must%20be%20a%20member%20of%20the%20same%20Google%20Workspace%20organization%20as%20the%20delegator%20user.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Manage%20delegates.%20Gmail%20imposes%20limitations%20on%20the%20number%20of%20delegates%20and%20delegators%20each%20user%20in%20a%20Google%20Workspace%20organization%20can%20have.%20These%20limits%20depend%20on%20your%20organization%2C%20but%20in%20general%20each%20user%20can%20have%20up%20to%2025%20delegates%20and%20up%20to%2010%20delegators.%20A%20delegate%20user%20must%20be%20referred%20to%20by%20their%20primary%20email%20address%2C%20and%20not%20an%20email%20alias.%20When%20a%20new%20delegate%20is%20created%2C%20there%20may%20be%20up%20to%20a%20one%20minute%20delay%20before%20the%20new%20delegate%20is%20available%20for%20use.%20This%20method%20is%20only%20available%20to%20service%20account%20clients%20that%20have%20been%20delegated%20domain-wide%20authority.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `users.settings.sendAs.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a custom "from" send-as alias. If an SMTP MSA is specified, Gmail will attempt to connect to the SMTP service to validate the configuration before creating the alias. If ownership verification is required for the alias, a message will be sent to the email address and the resource's verification status will be set to `pending`; otherwise, the resource will be created with verification status set to `accepted`. If a signature is provided, Gmail will sanitize the HTML before saving it with the alias. For more information, see Manage aliases and signatures with the Gmail API. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20gmail.users.settings.sendAs.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60gmail.users.settings.sendAs.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20custom%20%22from%22%20send-as%20alias.%20If%20an%20SMTP%20MSA%20is%20specified%2C%20Gmail%20will%20attempt%20to%20connect%20to%20the%20SMTP%20service%20to%20validate%20the%20configuration%20before%20creating%20the%20alias.%20If%20ownership%20verification%20is%20required%20for%20the%20alias%2C%20a%20message%20will%20be%20sent%20to%20the%20email%20address%20and%20the%20resource's%20verification%20status%20will%20be%20set%20to%20%60pending%60%3B%20otherwise%2C%20the%20resource%20will%20be%20created%20with%20verification%20status%20set%20to%20%60accepted%60.%20If%20a%20signature%20is%20provided%2C%20Gmail%20will%20sanitize%20the%20HTML%20before%20saving%20it%20with%20the%20alias.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Manage%20aliases%20and%20signatures%20with%20the%20Gmail%20API.%20This%20method%20is%20only%20available%20to%20service%20account%20clients%20that%20have%20been%20delegated%20domain-wide%20authority.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

## sheets

`manage_sheets` — 8 of 17 methods exposed. Google Discovery: `sheets:v4`.

| Method | | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `spreadsheets.batchUpdate`<br>*POST* | Applies one or more updates to the spreadsheet. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is not valid then the entire request will fail and nothing will be applied. Some requests have replies to give you some information about how they are applied. The replies will mirror the requests. For example, if you applied 4 updates and the 3rd one had a reply, then the response will have 2 empty replies, the actual reply, and another empty reply, in that order. Due to the collaborative nature of spreadsheets, it is not guaranteed that the spreadsheet will reflect exactly your changes after this completes, however it is guaranteed that the updates in the request will be applied together atomically. Your changes may be altered with respect to collaborator changes. If there are no collaborators, the spreadsheet should reflect your changes. | ✅ `manage_sheets addSheet, renameSheet, deleteSheet, duplicateSheet, renameSpreadsheet` |
| `spreadsheets.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a spreadsheet, returning the newly created spreadsheet. | ✅ `manage_sheets create` |
| `spreadsheets.developerMetadata.get`<br>*GET* | Returns the developer metadata with the specified ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and the developer metadata's unique metadataId. For more information, see Read, write, and search metadata. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20sheets.spreadsheets.developerMetadata.get&body=**Method%3A**%20%60sheets.spreadsheets.developerMetadata.get%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20the%20developer%20metadata%20with%20the%20specified%20ID.%20The%20caller%20must%20specify%20the%20spreadsheet%20ID%20and%20the%20developer%20metadata's%20unique%20metadataId.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Read%2C%20write%2C%20and%20search%20metadata.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spreadsheets.developerMetadata.search`<br>*POST* | Returns all developer metadata matching the specified DataFilter. For more information, see Read, write, and search metadata. If the provided DataFilter represents a DeveloperMetadataLookup object, this will return all DeveloperMetadata entries selected by it. If the DataFilter represents a location in a spreadsheet, this will return all developer metadata associated with locations intersecting that region. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20sheets.spreadsheets.developerMetadata.search&body=**Method%3A**%20%60sheets.spreadsheets.developerMetadata.search%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20all%20developer%20metadata%20matching%20the%20specified%20DataFilter.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Read%2C%20write%2C%20and%20search%20metadata.%20If%20the%20provided%20DataFilter%20represents%20a%20DeveloperMetadataLookup%20object%2C%20this%20will%20return%20all%20DeveloperMetadata%20entries%20selected%20by%20it.%20If%20the%20DataFilter%20represents%20a%20location%20in%20a%20spreadsheet%2C%20this%20will%20return%20all%20developer%20metadata%20associated%20with%20locations%20intersecting%20that%20region.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spreadsheets.get`<br>*GET* | Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID. By default, data within grids is not returned. You can include grid data in one of 2 ways: * Specify a field mask listing your desired fields using the `fields` URL parameter in HTTP * Set the includeGridData URL parameter to true. If a field mask is set, the `includeGridData` parameter is ignored For large spreadsheets, as a best practice, retrieve only the specific spreadsheet fields that you want. To retrieve only subsets of spreadsheet data, use the ranges URL parameter. Ranges are specified using A1 notation. You can define a single cell (for example, `A1`) or multiple cells (for example, `A1:D5`). You can also get cells from other sheets within the same spreadsheet (for example, `Sheet2!A1:C4`) or retrieve multiple ranges at once (for example, `?ranges=A1:D5&ranges=Sheet2!A1:C4`). Limiting the range returns only the portions of the spreadsheet that intersect the requested ranges. | ✅ `manage_sheets get` |
| `spreadsheets.getByDataFilter`<br>*POST* | Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID. For more information, see Read, write, and search metadata. This method differs from GetSpreadsheet in that it allows selecting which subsets of spreadsheet data to return by specifying a dataFilters parameter. Multiple DataFilters can be specified. Specifying one or more data filters returns the portions of the spreadsheet that intersect ranges matched by any of the filters. By default, data within grids is not returned. You can include grid data in one of two ways: * Specify a field mask listing your desired fields using the `fields` URL parameter in HTTP. * Set the includeGridData parameter to `true`. If a field mask is set, the `includeGridData` parameter is ignored. For large spreadsheets, as a best practice, retrieve only the specific spreadsheet fields that you want. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20sheets.spreadsheets.getByDataFilter&body=**Method%3A**%20%60sheets.spreadsheets.getByDataFilter%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20the%20spreadsheet%20at%20the%20given%20ID.%20The%20caller%20must%20specify%20the%20spreadsheet%20ID.%20For%20more%20information%2C%20see%20Read%2C%20write%2C%20and%20search%20metadata.%20This%20method%20differs%20from%20GetSpreadsheet%20in%20that%20it%20allows%20selecting%20which%20subsets%20of%20spreadsheet%20data%20to%20return%20by%20specifying%20a%20dataFilters%20parameter.%20Multiple%20DataFilters%20can%20be%20specified.%20Specifying%20one%20or%20more%20data%20filters%20returns%20the%20portions%20of%20the%20spreadsheet%20that%20intersect%20ranges%20matched%20by%20any%20of%20the%20filters.%20By%20default%2C%20data%20within%20grids%20is%20not%20returned.%20You%20can%20include%20grid%20data%20in%20one%20of%20two%20ways%3A%20*%20Specify%20a%20field%20mask%20listing%20your%20desired%20fields%20using%20the%20%60fields%60%20URL%20parameter%20in%20HTTP.%20*%20Set%20the%20includeGridData%20parameter%20to%20%60true%60.%20If%20a%20field%20mask%20is%20set%2C%20the%20%60includeGridData%60%20parameter%20is%20ignored.%20For%20large%20spreadsheets%2C%20as%20a%20best%20practice%2C%20retrieve%20only%20the%20specific%20spreadsheet%20fields%20that%20you%20want.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo`<br>*POST* | Copies a single sheet from a spreadsheet to another spreadsheet. Returns the properties of the newly created sheet. | ✅ `manage_sheets copySheetTo` |
| `spreadsheets.values.append`<br>*POST* | Appends values to a spreadsheet. The input range is used to search for existing data and find a "table" within that range. Values will be appended to the next row of the table, starting with the first column of the table. See the guide and sample code for specific details of how tables are detected and data is appended. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID, range, and a valueInputOption. The `valueInputOption` only controls how the input data will be added to the sheet (column-wise or row-wise), it does not influence what cell the data starts being written to. | ✅ `manage_sheets append` |

---

# Not targeted yet

Google publishes these too, and this server does not touch them. They are listed here because *not targeted* is a decision, not a fact of nature — and it was made without you.

Nothing here is wired up, so every method carries a **Request** link. A request that names a task the tool cannot do today is what moves one of these from this section into the one above.

Some are cheap and some are not, and the difference is worth knowing before you ask:

- **contacts (People API)** — straightforward. Ordinary OAuth scopes (`contacts`, `contacts.readonly`), works on a personal Google account, and "who is this person and how do I reach them" is a question agents ask constantly.
- **slides**, **forms** — small, self-contained surfaces. Slides is 5 methods; Forms is 10.
- **chat (Google Chat API)** — the biggest surface here and the least certain. Much of it is built for Chat **apps** (bots), not for acting as yourself: the scope lists are dominated by `chat.bot`, `chat.app.*` and `chat.admin.*`. User-credential access is a narrower path and, in places, Workspace-only — so a personal `@gmail.com` account may not be able to call it at all. That question would need answering with a real request before any of it is promised.

| `spaces.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a space. Can be used to create a named space, or a group chat in `Import mode`. For an example, see Create a space. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with administrator approval and one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces.create` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.create` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) When authenticating as an app, the `space.customer` field must be set in the request. When authenticating as an app, the Chat app is added as a member of the space. However, unlike human authentication, the Chat app is not added as a space manager. By default, the Chat app can be removed from the space by all space members. To allow only space managers to remove the app from a space, set `space.permission_settings.manage_apps` to `managers_allowed`. Space membership upon creation depends on whether the space is created in `Import mode`: * **Import mode:** No members are created. * **All other modes:** The calling user is added as a member. This is: * The app itself when using app authentication. * The human user when using user authentication. If you receive the error message `ALREADY_EXISTS` when creating a space, try a different `displayName`. An existing space within the Google Workspace organization might already use this display name. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20space.%20Can%20be%20used%20to%20create%20a%20named%20space%2C%20or%20a%20group%20chat%20in%20%60Import%20mode%60.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Create%20a%20space.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20administrator%20approval%20and%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.spaces.create%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.spaces%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces.create%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20When%20authenticating%20as%20an%20app%2C%20the%20%60space.customer%60%20field%20must%20be%20set%20in%20the%20request.%20When%20authenticating%20as%20an%20app%2C%20the%20Chat%20app%20is%20added%20as%20a%20member%20of%20the%20space.%20However%2C%20unlike%20human%20authentication%2C%20the%20Chat%20app%20is%20not%20added%20as%20a%20space%20manager.%20By%20default%2C%20the%20Chat%20app%20can%20be%20removed%20from%20the%20space%20by%20all%20space%20members.%20To%20allow%20only%20space%20managers%20to%20remove%20the%20app%20from%20a%20space%2C%20set%20%60space.permission_settings.manage_apps%60%20to%20%60managers_allowed%60.%20Space%20membership%20upon%20creation%20depends%20on%20whether%20the%20space%20is%20created%20in%20%60Import%20mode%60%3A%20*%20**Import%20mode%3A**%20No%20members%20are%20created.%20*%20**All%20other%20modes%3A**%20The%20calling%20user%20is%20added%20as%20a%20member.%20This%20is%3A%20*%20The%20app%20itself%20when%20using%20app%20authentication.%20*%20The%20human%20user%20when%20using%20user%20authentication.%20If%20you%20receive%20the%20error%20message%20%60ALREADY_EXISTS%60%20when%20creating%20a%20space%2C%20try%20a%20different%20%60displayName%60.%20An%20existing%20space%20within%20the%20Google%20Workspace%20organization%20might%20already%20use%20this%20display%20name.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.delete`<br>*DELETE* | Deletes a named space. Always performs a cascading delete, which means that the space's child resources—like messages posted in the space and memberships in the space—are also deleted. For an example, see Delete a space. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with administrator approval and the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.delete` (only in spaces the app created) - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.delete` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) - User authentication grants administrator privileges when an administrator account authenticates, `use_admin_access` is `true`, and the following authorization scope is used: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.delete` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.delete&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.delete%60%20(%60DELETE%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Deletes%20a%20named%20space.%20Always%20performs%20a%20cascading%20delete%2C%20which%20means%20that%20the%20space's%20child%20resources%E2%80%94like%20messages%20posted%20in%20the%20space%20and%20memberships%20in%20the%20space%E2%80%94are%20also%20deleted.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Delete%20a%20space.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20administrator%20approval%20and%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.delete%60%20(only%20in%20spaces%20the%20app%20created)%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.delete%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20-%20User%20authentication%20grants%20administrator%20privileges%20when%20an%20administrator%20account%20authenticates%2C%20%60use_admin_access%60%20is%20%60true%60%2C%20and%20the%20following%20authorization%20scope%20is%20used%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.admin.delete%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.findDirectMessage`<br>*GET* | Returns the existing direct message with the specified user. If no direct message space is found, returns a `404 NOT_FOUND` error. For an example, see Find a direct message. With app authentication, returns the direct message space between the specified user and the calling Chat app. With user authentication, returns the direct message space between the specified user and the authenticated user. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.findDirectMessage&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.findDirectMessage%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20the%20existing%20direct%20message%20with%20the%20specified%20user.%20If%20no%20direct%20message%20space%20is%20found%2C%20returns%20a%20%60404%20NOT_FOUND%60%20error.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Find%20a%20direct%20message.%20With%20app%20authentication%2C%20returns%20the%20direct%20message%20space%20between%20the%20specified%20user%20and%20the%20calling%20Chat%20app.%20With%20user%20authentication%2C%20returns%20the%20direct%20message%20space%20between%20the%20specified%20user%20and%20the%20authenticated%20user.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.bot%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.findGroupChats`<br>*GET* | Returns all spaces with `spaceType == GROUP_CHAT`, whose human memberships contain exactly the calling user, and the users specified in `FindGroupChatsRequest.users`. Only members that have joined the conversation are supported. For an example, see Find group chats. If the calling user blocks, or is blocked by, some users, and no spaces with the entire specified set of users are found, this method returns spaces that don't include the blocked or blocking users. The specified set of users must contain only human (non-app) memberships. A request that contains non-human users doesn't return any spaces. Requires user authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.findGroupChats&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.findGroupChats%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20all%20spaces%20with%20%60spaceType%20%3D%3D%20GROUP_CHAT%60%2C%20whose%20human%20memberships%20contain%20exactly%20the%20calling%20user%2C%20and%20the%20users%20specified%20in%20%60FindGroupChatsRequest.users%60.%20Only%20members%20that%20have%20joined%20the%20conversation%20are%20supported.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Find%20group%20chats.%20If%20the%20calling%20user%20blocks%2C%20or%20is%20blocked%20by%2C%20some%20users%2C%20and%20no%20spaces%20with%20the%20entire%20specified%20set%20of%20users%20are%20found%2C%20this%20method%20returns%20spaces%20that%20don't%20include%20the%20blocked%20or%20blocking%20users.%20The%20specified%20set%20of%20users%20must%20contain%20only%20human%20(non-app)%20memberships.%20A%20request%20that%20contains%20non-human%20users%20doesn't%20return%20any%20spaces.%20Requires%20user%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.memberships.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.memberships%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.get`<br>*GET* | Returns details about a space. For an example, see Get details about a space. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces` with administrator approval - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` - User authentication grants administrator privileges when an administrator account rpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) | `spaces.members.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a membership for the calling Chat app, a user, or a Google Group. Creating memberships for other Chat apps isn't supported. When creating a membership, if the specified member has their auto-accept policy turned off, then they're invited, and must accept the space invitation before joining. Otherwise, creating a membership adds the member directly to the specified space. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with administrator approval and the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.memberships` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships.app` (to add the calling app to the space) - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) - User authentication grants administrator privileges when an administrator account authenticates, `use_admin_access` is `true`, and the following authorization scope is used: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.memberships` App authentication is not supported for the following use cases: - Inviting users external to the Workspace organization that owns the space. - Adding a Google Group to a space. - Adding a Chat app to a space. For example usage, see: - Invite or add a user to a space. - Invite or add a Google Group to a space. - Add the Chat app to a space. | e%20or%20add%20a%20user%20to%20a%20space.%20-%20Invite%20or%20add%20a%20Google%20Group%20to%20a%20space.%20-%20Add%20the%20Chat%20app%20to%20a%20space.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) | `spaces.members.delete`<br>*DELETE* | Deletes a membership. For an example, see Remove a user or a Google Chat app from a space. If you're using app authentication the Chat app must be the space creator. | | `spaces.members.patch`<br>*PATCH* | Updates a membership. For an example, see Update a user's membership in a space. User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) - User authentication grants administrator privileges when an administrator account authenticates, `use_admin_access` is `true`, and the following authorization scope is used: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.memberships` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.members.patch&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.members.patch%60%20(%60PATCH%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Updates%20a%20membership.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Update%20a%20user's%20membership%20in%20a%20space.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20administrator%20approval%20and%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.memberships%60%20(only%20in%20spaces%20the%20app%20created)%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.memberships%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20-%20User%20authentication%20grants%20administrator%20privileges%20when%20an%20administrator%20account%20authenticates%2C%20%60use_admin_access%60%20is%20%60true%60%2C%20and%20the%20following%20authorization%20scope%20is%20used%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.admin.memberships%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `spaces.messages.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a message in a Google Chat space. For an example, see Send a message. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.create` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) Chat attributes the message sender differently depending on the type of authentication that you use in your request. The following image shows how Chat attributes a message when you use app authentication. Chat displays the Chat app as the message sender. The content of the message can contain text (`text`), cards (`cardsV2`), and accessory widgets (`accessoryWidgets`). !Message sent with app authentication The following image shows how Chat attributes a message when you use user authentication. Chat displays the user as the message sender and attributes the Chat app to the message by displaying its name. The content of message can only contain text (`text`). !Message sent with user authentication The maximum message size, including the message contents, is 32,000 bytes. For webhook requests, the response doesn't contain the full message. The response only populates the `name` and `thread.name` fields in addition to the information that was in the request. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.messages.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.messages.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20message%20in%20a%20Google%20Chat%20space.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Send%20a%20message.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.bot%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.create%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20Chat%20attributes%20the%20message%20sender%20differently%20depending%20on%20the%20type%20of%20authentication%20that%20you%20use%20in%20your%20request.%20The%20following%20image%20shows%20how%20Chat%20attributes%20a%20message%20when%20you%20use%20app%20authentication.%20Chat%20displays%20the%20Chat%20app%20as%20the%20message%20sender.%20The%20content%20of%20the%20message%20can%20contain%20text%20(%60text%60)%2C%20cards%20(%60cardsV2%60)%2C%20and%20accessory%20widgets%20(%60accessoryWidgets%60).%20!Message%20sent%20with%20app%20authentication%20The%20following%20image%20shows%20how%20Chat%20attributes%20a%20message%20when%20you%20use%20user%20authentication.%20Chat%20displays%20the%20user%20as%20the%20message%20sender%20and%20attributes%20the%20Chat%20app%20to%20the%20message%20by%20displaying%20its%20name.%20The%20content%20of%20message%20can%20only%20contain%20text%20(%60text%60).%20!Message%20sent%20with%20user%20authentication%20The%20maximum%20message%20size%2C%20including%20the%20message%20contents%2C%20is%2032%2C000%20bytes.%20For%20webhook%20requests%2C%20the%20response%20doesn't%20contain%20the%20full%20message.%20The%20response%20only%20populates%20the%20%60name%60%20and%20%60thread.name%60%20fields%20in%20addition%20to%20the%20information%20that%20was%20in%20the%20request.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) When using app authentication, requests can only delete messages created by the calling Chat app. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.messages.delete&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.messages.delete%60%20(%60DELETE%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Deletes%20a%20message.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Delete%20a%20message.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.bot%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20When%20using%20app%20authentication%2C%20requests%20can%20only%20delete%20messages%20created%20by%20the%20calling%20Chat%20app.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.messages.get`<br>*GET* | Returns details about a message. For an example, see Get details about a message. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot`: When using this authorization scope, this method returns details about a message the Chat app has access to, like direct messages and slash commands that invoke the Chat app. - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.messages.readonly` with administrator approval. When using this authentication scope, this method returns details about a public message in a space. - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` Note: Might return a message from a blocked member or space. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.messages.get&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.messages.get%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20details%20about%20a%20message.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Get%20details%20about%20a%20message.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.bot%60%3A%20When%20using%20this%20authorization%20scope%2C%20this%20method%20returns%20details%20about%20a%20message%20the%20Chat%20app%20has%20access%20to%2C%20like%20direct%20messages%20and%20slash%20commands%20that%20invoke%20the%20Chat%20app.%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.messages.readonly%60%20with%20administrator%20approval.%20When%20using%20this%20authentication%20scope%2C%20this%20method%20returns%20details%20about%20a%20public%20message%20in%20a%20space.%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20Note%3A%20Might%20return%20a%20message%20from%20a%20blocked%20member%20or%20space.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.messages.list`<br>*GET* | Lists messages in a space that the caller is a member of, including messages from blocked members and spaces. System messages, like those announcing new space members, aren't included. If you list messages from a space with no messages, the response is an empty object. When using a REST/HTTP interface, the response contains an empty JSON object, `{}`. For an example, see List messages. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with administrator approval with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.messages.readonly`. When using this authentication scope, this method only returns public messages in a space. It doesn't include private messages. - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.messages.list&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.messages.list%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Lists%20messages%20in%20a%20space%20that%20the%20caller%20is%20a%20member%20of%2C%20including%20messages%20from%20blocked%20members%20and%20spaces.%20System%20messages%2C%20like%20those%20announcing%20new%20space%20members%2C%20aren't%20included.%20If%20you%20list%20messages%20from%20a%20space%20with%20no%20messages%2C%20the%20response%20is%20an%20empty%20object.%20When%20using%20a%20REST%2FHTTP%20interface%2C%20the%20response%20contains%20an%20empty%20JSON%20object%2C%20%60%7B%7D%60.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20List%20messages.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20administrator%20approval%20with%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.messages.readonly%60.%20When%20using%20this%20authentication%20scope%2C%20this%20method%20only%20returns%20public%20messages%20in%20a%20space.%20It%20doesn't%20include%20private%20messages.%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) | `spaces.messages.patch`<br>*PATCH* | Updates a message. There's a difference between the `patch` and `update` methods. The `patch` method uses a `patch` request while the `update` method uses a `put` request. We recommend using the `patch` method. For an example, see Update a message. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - | `spaces.messages.update`<br>*PUT* | Updates a message. There's a difference between the `patch` and `update` methods. The `patch` method uses a `patch` request while the `update` method uses a `put` request. We recommend using the `patch` method. For an example, see Update a message. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with the authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.bot` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) When using app authentication, requests can only update messages created by the calling Chat app. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.messages.update&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.messages.update%60%20(%60PUT%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Updates%20a%20message.%20There's%20a%20difference%20between%20the%20%60patch%60%20and%20%60update%60%20methods.%20The%20%60patch%60%20method%20uses%20a%20%60patch%60%20request%20while%20the%20%60update%60%20method%20uses%20a%20%60put%60%20request.%20We%20recommend%20using%20the%20%60patch%60%20method.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Update%20a%20message.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20the%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.bot%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.import%60%20(import%20mode%20spaces%20only)%20When%20using%20app%20authentication%2C%20requests%20can%20only%20update%20messages%20created%20by%20the%20calling%20Chat%20app.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.patch`<br>*PATCH* | Updates a space. For an example, see Update a space. If you're updating the `displayName` field and receive the error message `ALREADY_EXISTS`, try a different display name.. An existing space within the Google Workspace organization might already use this display name. Supports the following types of authentication: - App authentication with administrator approval and one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.import` (import mode spaces only) - User authentication grants administrator privileges when an administrator account authenticates, `use_admin_access` is `true`, and the following authorization scopes is used: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.spaces` App authentication has the following limitations: - To update either `space.predefined_permission_settings` or `space.permission_settings`, the app must be the space creator. - Updating the `space.access_settings.audience` is not supported for app authentication. | 0cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) | `spaces.search`<br>*GET* | Returns a list of spaces in a Google Workspace organization. For an example, see Search for and manage spaces. When `use_admin_access` is set to `false`, the results are limited to spaces where the calling user is a joined member. To search with administrator privileges, set `use_admin_access` to `true`. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` - User authentication with administrator privileges and one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.spaces.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.admin.spaces` |
