| `spaces.setup`<br>*POST* | Creates a space and adds specified users to it. The calling user is automatically added to the space, and shouldn't be specified as a membership in the request. For an example, see Set up a space with initial members. To specify the human members to add, add memberships with the appropriate `membership.member.name`. To add a human user, use `users/{user}`, where `{user}` can be the email address for the user. For users in the same Workspace organization `{user}` can also be the `id` for the person from the People API, or the `id` for the user in the Directory API. For example, if the People API Person profile ID for `user@example.com` is `123456789`, you can add the user to the space by setting the `membership.member.name` to `users/user@example.com` or `users/123456789`. To specify the Google groups to add, add memberships with the appropriate `membership.group_member.name`. To add or invite a Google group, use `groups/{group}`, where `{group}` is the `id` for the group from the Cloud Identity Groups API. For example, you can use Cloud Identity Groups lookup API to retrieve the ID `123456789` for group email `group@example.com`, then you can add the group to the space by setting the `membership.group_member.name` to `groups/123456789`. Group email is not supported, and Google groups can only be added as members in named spaces. For a named space or group chat, if the caller blocks, or is blocked by some members, or doesn't have permission to add some members, then those members aren't added to the created space. To create a direct message (DM) between the calling user and another human user, specify exactly one membership to represent the human user. If one user blocks the other, the request fails and the DM isn't created. To create a DM between the calling user and the calling app, set `Space.singleUserBotDm` to `true` and don't specify any memberships. You can only use this method to set up a DM with the calling app. To add the calling app as a member of a space or an existing DM between two human users, see Invite or add a user or app to a space. If a DM already exists between two users, even when one user blocks the other at the time a request is made, then the existing DM is returned. Spaces with threaded replies aren't supported. If you receive the error message `ALREADY_EXISTS` when setting up a space, try a different `displayName`. An existing space within the Google Workspace organization might already use this display name. Requires user authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.create` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.setup&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.setup%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20space%20and%20adds%20specified%20users%20to%20it.%20The%20calling%20user%20is%20automatically%20added%20to%20the%20space%2C%20and%20shouldn't%20be%20specified%20as%20a%20membership%20in%20the%20request.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Set%20up%20a%20space%20with%20initial%20members.%20To%20specify%20the%20human%20members%20to%20add%2C%20add%20memberships%20with%20the%20appropriate%20%60membership.member.name%60.%20To%20add%20a%20human%20user%2C%20use%20%60users%2F%7Buser%7D%60%2C%20where%20%60%7Buser%7D%60%20can%20be%20the%20email%20address%20for%20the%20user.%20For%20users%20in%20the%20same%20Workspace%20organization%20%60%7Buser%7D%60%20can%20also%20be%20the%20%60id%60%20for%20the%20person%20from%20the%20People%20API%2C%20or%20the%20%60id%60%20for%20the%20user%20in%20the%20Directory%20API.%20For%20example%2C%20if%20the%20People%20API%20Person%20profile%20ID%20for%20%60user%40example.com%60%20is%20%60123456789%60%2C%20you%20can%20add%20the%20user%20to%20the%20space%20by%20setting%20the%20%60membership.member.name%60%20to%20%60users%2Fuser%40example.com%60%20or%20%60users%2F123456789%60.%20To%20specify%20the%20Google%20groups%20to%20add%2C%20add%20memberships%20with%20the%20appropriate%20%60membership.group_member.name%60.%20To%20add%20or%20invite%20a%20Google%20group%2C%20use%20%60groups%2F%7Bgroup%7D%60%2C%20where%20%60%7Bgroup%7D%60%20is%20the%20%60id%60%20for%20the%20group%20from%20the%20Cloud%20Identity%20Groups%20API.%20For%20example%2C%20you%20can%20use%20Cloud%20Identity%20Groups%20lookup%20API%20to%20retrieve%20the%20ID%20%60123456789%60%20for%20group%20email%20%60group%40example.com%60%2C%20then%20you%20can%20add%20the%20group%20to%20the%20space%20by%20setting%20the%20%60membership.group_member.name%60%20to%20%60groups%2F123456789%60.%20Group%20email%20is%20not%20supported%2C%20and%20Google%20groups%20can%20only%20be%20added%20as%20members%20in%20named%20spaces.%20For%20a%20named%20space%20or%20group%20chat%2C%20if%20the%20caller%20blocks%2C%20or%20is%20blocked%20by%20some%20members%2C%20or%20doesn't%20have%20permission%20to%20add%20some%20members%2C%20then%20those%20members%20aren't%20added%20to%20the%20created%20space.%20To%20create%20a%20direct%20message%20(DM)%20between%20the%20calling%20user%20and%20another%20human%20user%2C%20specify%20exactly%20one%20membership%20to%20represent%20the%20human%20user.%20If%20one%20user%20blocks%20the%20other%2C%20the%20request%20fails%20and%20the%20DM%20isn't%20created.%20To%20create%20a%20DM%20between%20the%20calling%20user%20and%20the%20calling%20app%2C%20set%20%60Space.singleUserBotDm%60%20to%20%60true%60%20and%20don't%20specify%20any%20memberships.%20You%20can%20only%20use%20this%20method%20to%20set%20up%20a%20DM%20with%20the%20calling%20app.%20To%20add%20the%20calling%20app%20as%20a%20member%20of%20a%20space%20or%20an%20existing%20DM%20between%20two%20human%20users%2C%20see%20Invite%20or%20add%20a%20user%20or%20app%20to%20a%20space.%20If%20a%20DM%20already%20exists%20between%20two%20users%2C%20even%20when%20one%20user%20blocks%20the%20other%20at%20the%20time%20a%20request%20is%20made%2C%20then%20the%20existing%20DM%20is%20returned.%20Spaces%20with%20threaded%20replies%20aren't%20supported.%20If%20you%20receive%20the%20error%20message%20%60ALREADY_EXISTS%60%20when%20setting%20up%20a%20space%2C%20try%20a%20different%20%60displayName%60.%20An%20existing%20space%20within%20the%20Google%20Workspace%20organization%20might%20already%20use%20this%20display%20name.%20Requires%20user%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces.create%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `spaces.spaceEvents.get`<br>*GET* | Returns an event from a Google Chat space. The event payload contains the most recent version of the resource that changed. For example, if you request an event about a new message but the message was later updated, the server returns the updated `Message` resource in the event payload. Note: The `permissionSettings` field is not returned in the Space object of the Space event data for this request. Supports the following types of authentication with an authorization scope appropriate for reading the requested data: - App authentication with administrator approval with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.spaces.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.messages.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.memberships` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.app.memberships.readonly` - User authentication with one of the following authorization scopes: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.reactions.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.reactions` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships.readonly` - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.memberships` To get an event, the authenticated caller must be a member of the space. For an example, see Get details about an event from a Google Chat space. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.spaces.spaceEvents.get&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.spaces.spaceEvents.get%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Returns%20an%20event%20from%20a%20Google%20Chat%20space.%20The%20event%20payload%20contains%20the%20most%20recent%20version%20of%20the%20resource%20that%20changed.%20For%20example%2C%20if%20you%20request%20an%20event%20about%20a%20new%20message%20but%20the%20message%20was%20later%20updated%2C%20the%20server%20returns%20the%20updated%20%60Message%60%20resource%20in%20the%20event%20payload.%20Note%3A%20The%20%60permissionSettings%60%20field%20is%20not%20returned%20in%20the%20Space%20object%20of%20the%20Space%20event%20data%20for%20this%20request.%20Supports%20the%20following%20types%20of%20authentication%20with%20an%20authorization%20scope%20appropriate%20for%20reading%20the%20requested%20data%3A%20-%20App%20authentication%20with%20administrator%20approval%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.spaces%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.spaces.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.messages.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.memberships%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.app.memberships.readonly%60%20-%20User%20authentication%20with%20one%20of%20the%20following%20authorization%20scopes%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.spaces%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.reactions.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.messages.reactions%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.memberships.readonly%60%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.memberships%60%20To%20get%20an%20event%2C%20the%20authenticated%20caller%20must%20be%20a%20member%20of%20the%20space.%20For%20an%20example%2C%20see%20Get%20details%20about%20an%20event%20from%20a%20Google%20Chat%20space.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) | `spaces.spaceEvents.list`<br>*GET* | Lists events from a Google Chat space. For each event, the payload contains the most recent version of the Chat resource. For an example, see List events from a Google Chat space. | | `users.availability.get`<br>*GET* | Returns availability information for a human user in Google Chat. | `users.availability.markAsActive`<br>*POST* | Marks user as `ACTIVE` in Google Chat. Sets the user's availability state to `ACTIVE`. The `ACTIVE` state lasts until the specified expiration, at which point the user's state becomes `AWAY`. Note that if the user is actively using Chat, the `ACTIVE` state duration may extend beyond the provided expiration. This method only updates the authenticated user's availability. Requires user authentication with authorization scope: - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.users.availability` | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20chat.users.availability.markAsActive&body=**Method%3A**%20%60chat.users.availability.markAsActive%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Marks%20user%20as%20%60ACTIVE%60%20in%20Google%20Chat.%20Sets%20the%20user's%20availability%20state%20to%20%60ACTIVE%60.%20The%20%60ACTIVE%60%20state%20lasts%20until%20the%20specified%20expiration%2C%20at%20which%20point%20the%20user's%20state%20becomes%20%60AWAY%60.%20Note%20that%20if%20the%20user%20is%20actively%20using%20Chat%2C%20the%20%60ACTIVE%60%20state%20duration%20may%20extend%20beyond%20the%20provided%20expiration.%20This%20method%20only%20updates%20the%20authenticated%20user's%20availability.%20Requires%20user%20authentication%20with%20authorization%20scope%3A%20-%20%60https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fchat.users.availability%60%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

| `otherContacts.list`<br>*GET* | List all "Other contacts", that is contacts that are not in a contact group. "Other contacts" are typically auto created contacts from interactions. Sync tokens expire 7 days after the full sync. A request with an expired sync token will get an error with an google.rpc.ErrorInfo with reason "EXPIRED_SYNC_TOKEN". In the case of such an error clients should make a full sync request without a `sync_token`. The first page of a full sync request has an additional quota. If the quota is exceeded, a 429 error will be returned. This quota is fixed and can not be increased. When the `sync_token` is specified, resources deleted since the last sync will be returned as a person with `PersonMetadata.deleted` set to true. When the `page_token` or `sync_token` is specified, all other request parameters must match the first call. Writes may have a propagation delay of several minutes for sync requests. Incremental syncs are not intended for read-after-write use cases. See example usage at List the user's other contacts that have changed. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20people.otherContacts.list&body=**Method%3A**%20%60people.otherContacts.list%60%20(%60GET%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20List%20all%20%22Other%20contacts%22%2C%20that%20is%20contacts%20that%20are%20not%20in%20a%20contact%20group.%20%22Other%20contacts%22%20are%20typically%20auto%20created%20contacts%20from%20interactions.%20Sync%20tokens%20expire%207%20days%20after%20the%20full%20sync.%20A%20request%20with%20an%20expired%20sync%20token%20will%20get%20an%20error%20with%20an%20google.rpc.ErrorInfo%20with%20reason%20%22EXPIRED_SYNC_TOKEN%22.%20In%20the%20case%20of%20such%20an%20error%20clients%20should%20make%20a%20full%20sync%20request%20without%20a%20%60sync_token%60.%20The%20first%20page%20of%20a%20full%20sync%20request%20has%20an%20additional%20quota.%20If%20the%20quota%20is%20exceeded%2C%20a%20429%20error%20will%20be%20returned.%20This%20quota%20is%20fixed%20and%20can%20not%20be%20increased.%20When%20the%20%60sync_token%60%20is%20specified%2C%20resources%20deleted%20since%20the%20last%20sync%20will%20be%20returned%20as%20a%20person%20with%20%60PersonMetadata.deleted%60%20set%20to%20true.%20When%20the%20%60page_token%60%20or%20%60sync_token%60%20is%20specified%2C%20all%20other%20request%20parameters%20must%20match%20the%20first%20call.%20Writes%20may%20have%20a%20propagation%20delay%20of%20several%20minutes%20for%20sync%20requests.%20Incremental%20syncs%20are%20not%20intended%20for%20read-after-write%20use%20cases.%20See%20example%20usage%20at%20List%20the%20user's%20other%20contacts%20that%20have%20changed.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94% | `people.listDirectoryPeople`<br>*GET* | Provides a list of domain profiles and domain contacts in the authenticated user's domain directory. | `people.updateContact`<br>*PATCH* | Update contact data for an existing contact person. Any non-contact data will not be modified. Any non-contact data in the person to update will be ignored. All fields specified in the `update_mask` will be replaced. The server returns a 400 error if `person.metadata.sources` is not specified for the contact to be updated or if there is no contact source. The server returns a 400 error with reason `"failedPrecondition"` if `person.metadata.sources.etag` is different than the contact's etag, which indicates the contact has changed since its data was read. Clients should get the latest person and merge their updates into the latest person. If making sequential updates to the same person, the etag from the `updateContact` response should be used to avoid failures. The server returns a 400 error if `memberships` are being updated and there are no contact group memberships specified on the person. The server returns a 400 error if more than one field is specified on a field that is a singleton for contact sources: * biographies * birthdays * genders * names Mutate requests for the same user should be sent sequentially to avoid increased latency and failures. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20people.people.updateContact&body=**Method%3A**%20%60people.people.updateContact%60%20(%60PATCH%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Update%20contact%20data%20for%20an%20existing%20contact%20person.%20Any%20non-contact%20data%20will%20not%20be%20modified.%20Any%20non-contact%20data%20in%20the%20person%20to%20update%20will%20be%20ignored.%20All%20fields%20specified%20in%20the%20%60update_mask%60%20will%20be%20replaced.%20The%20server%20returns%20a%20400%20error%20if%20%60person.metadata.sources%60%20is%20not%20specified%20for%20the%20contact%20to%20be%20updated%20or%20if%20there%20is%20no%20contact%20source.%20The%20server%20returns%20a%20400%20error%20with%20reason%20%60%22failedPrecondition%22%60%20if%20%60person.metadata.sources.etag%60%20is%20different%20than%20the%20contact's%20etag%2C%20which%20indicates%20the%20contact%20has%20changed%20since%20its%20data%20was%20read.%20Clients%20should%20get%20the%20latest%20person%20and%20merge%20their%20updates%20into%20the%20latest%20person.%20If%20making%20sequential%20updates%20to%20the%20same%20person%2C%20the%20etag%20from%20the%20%60updateContact%60%20response%20should%20be%20used%20to%20avoid%20failures.%20The%20server%20returns%20a%20400%20error%20if%20%60memberships%60%20are%20being%20updated%20and%20there%20are%20no%20contact%20group%20memberships%20specified%20on%20the%20person.%20The%20server%20returns%20a%20400%20error%20if%20more%20than%20one%20field%20is%20specified%20on%20a%20field%20that%20is%20a%20singleton%20for%20contact%20sources%3A%20*%20biographies%20*%20birthdays%20*%20genders%20*%20names%20Mutate%20requests%20for%20the%20same%20user%20should%20be%20sent%20sequentially%20to%20avoid%20increased%20latency%20and%20failures.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |

## slides <a id="slides-not-targeted"></a>

Not exposed — 5 methods. Google Discovery: `slides:v1`.

| Method | | |
|---|---|---|
| `presentations.batchUpdate`<br>*POST* | Applies one or more updates to the presentation. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is not valid, then the entire request will fail and nothing will be applied. Some requests have replies to give you some information about how they are applied. Other requests do not need to return information; these each return an empty reply. The order of replies matches that of the requests. For example, suppose you call batchUpdate with four updates, and only the third one returns information. The response would have two empty replies: the reply to the third request, and another empty reply, in that order. Because other users may be editing the presentation, the presentation might not exactly reflect your changes: your changes may be altered with respect to collaborator changes. If there are no collaborators, the presentation should reflect your changes. In any case, the updates in your request are guaranteed to be applied together atomically. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20slides.presentations.batchUpdate&body=**Method%3A**%20%60slides.presentations.batchUpdate%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Applies%20one%20or%20more%20updates%20to%20the%20presentation.%20Each%20request%20is%20validated%20before%20being%20applied.%20If%20any%20request%20is%20not%20valid%2C%20then%20the%20entire%20request%20will%20fail%20and%20nothing%20will%20be%20applied.%20Some%20requests%20have%20replies%20to%20give%20you%20some%20information%20about%20how%20they%20are%20applied.%20Other%20requests%20do%20not%20need%20to%20return%20information%3B%20these%20each%20return%20an%20empty%20reply.%20The%20order%20of%20replies%20matches%20that%20of%20the%20requests.%20For%20example%2C%20suppose%20you%20call%20batchUpdate%20with%20four%20updates%2C%20and%20only%20the%20third%20one%20returns%20information.%20The%20response%20would%20have%20two%20empty%20replies%3A%20the%20reply%20to%20the%20third%20request%2C%20and%20another%20empty%20reply%2C%20in%20that%20order.%20Because%20other%20users%20may%20be%20editing%20the%20presentation%2C%20the%20presentation%20might%20not%20exactly%20reflect%20your%20changes%3A%20your%20changes%20may%20be%20altered%20with%20respect%20to%20collaborator%20changes.%20If%20there%20are%20no%20collaborators%2C%20the%20presentation%20should%20reflect%20your%20changes.%20In%20any%20case%2C%20the%20updates%20in%20your%20request%20are%20guaranteed%20to%20be%20applied%20together%20atomically.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
| `presentations.create`<br>*POST* | Creates a blank presentation using the title given in the request. If a `presentationId` is provided, it is used as the ID of the new presentation. Otherwise, a new ID is generated. Other fields in the request, including any provided content, are ignored. Returns the created presentation. | [Request](https://github.com/aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp/issues/new?title=Expose%20slides.presentations.create&body=**Method%3A**%20%60slides.presentations.create%60%20(%60POST%60)%0A%0A**Google's%20description%3A**%20Creates%20a%20blank%20presentation%20using%20the%20title%20given%20in%20the%20request.%20If%20a%20%60presentationId%60%20is%20provided%2C%20it%20is%20used%20as%20the%20ID%20of%20the%20new%20presentation.%20Otherwise%2C%20a%20new%20ID%20is%20generated.%20Other%20fields%20in%20the%20request%2C%20including%20any%20provided%20content%2C%20are%20ignored.%20Returns%20the%20created%20presentation.%0A%0A%23%23%23%20What%20do%20you%20want%20to%20do%20that%20you%20can't%20do%20today%3F%0A%0A_Describe%20the%20task%2C%20not%20the%20method.%20What%20are%20you%20trying%20to%20get%20the%20agent%20to%20accomplish%3F_%0A%0A%23%23%23%20Why%20does%20an%20existing%20operation%20not%20cover%20it%3F%0A%0A_The%20server%20exposes%20a%20curated%20subset%20on%20purpose%20%E2%80%94%20more%20surface%20is%20not%20better.%20What%20did%20you%20try%2C%20and%20where%20did%20it%20fall%20short%3F_%0A&labels=enhancement) |
